status: Fixed in 2026-06-28 onefile extraction lock lane
finding: Running `npm run build` and a frozen-runner command in parallel can expose a transient onefile cache path failure.
expectedBehavior: Frozen runner cache refresh should be atomic or runner commands should wait/retry when the cache is being rebuilt.
evidenceOrRepro: Repaired on 2026-06-28 by moving the onefile launcher to an atomic extraction protocol: payload extraction now takes a per-payload lock, writes into a unique staging directory, publishes readiness only after the payload is complete, and lets concurrent runners wait/retry against the same cache root instead of crashing on a half-refreshed tree. The launcher also now honors `ATM_ONEFILE_CACHE_ROOT`, which makes isolated validation and cache hygiene deterministic. Regression coverage lives in `scripts/validate-onefile-release.ts`, and `scripts/validate-onefile-budget.ts --json` now also reports extraction-lock handoff results alongside startup timing.
followUp: Keep extraction-lock wait coverage in `scripts/validate-onefile-release.ts` and preserve the lock-handoff summary in `scripts/validate-onefile-budget.ts`; future onefile cache changes must not reintroduce partial-tree visibility during rebuild.
